  "findings": [
    "Cotter pin omitted from castle nut on suspension/steering joint",
    "Castle nut can back off under vibration, leading to joint separation"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Immediately quarantine the affected assembly or unit",
    "Install correct cotter pin and verify torque on castle nut per OEM spec",
    "Re-inspect all similar joints on this station and upstream for missing cotter pins"
  ],
  "rationale": "A missing cotter pin on a castle nut is a critical safety defect. The pin is a positive locking device required by OEM assembly standards and basic automotive practice; without it the nut can loosen under road vibration, ultimately causing separation of a steering or suspension joint and loss of vehicle control. Even at rough-complete stage this must be rejected.",
  "references": [
    "OEM assembly specification for suspension/steering fasteners",
    "General torque and locking requirements per standard automotive alignment and fastener integrity practices"
  ]
}
